title: Implement schema evolution and smart routing
markdown:
## Goal

Implement schema evolution handling (detect version mismatches, teardown, setup, and backfill) and smart routing via `inferViewFromUri()` that routes read queries to the appropriate indexer view.

## Background

Indexer definitions have a `version` field. When the version changes (new fields, changed types, removed fields), the existing index is stale. The evolution system must detect this mismatch and orchestrate a clean rebuild: teardown the old index, create the new one, and backfill from source data.

Smart routing allows a `FunctionalClient` decorator to intercept `read()` and `list()` calls and route them to an indexer when the URI matches an indexed pattern, falling back to the source node for non-indexed data.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `initializeIndexer(options)` function:
  - `definition: IndexerDefinition`
  - `backend: IndexerBackend`
  - `source: NodeProtocolInterface`
  - `cursorStore: CursorStore`
  - Checks stored version against `definition.version`
  - If mismatch: `backend.teardown()` -> `backend.initialize(definition)` -> `backfillIndexer()` -> update stored version
  - If match: no-op (indexer is up to date)
  - Returns `{ action: "initialized" | "rebuilt" | "unchanged"; version: number }`
- [ ] Version stored at `mutable://indexer/{name}/version` via the cursor store or a dedicated metadata store
- [ ] `inferViewFromUri(uri: string, indexers: IndexerDefinition[])`:
  - Returns the indexer definition whose URI patterns match the given URI
  - Returns `null` if no indexer matches
- [ ] `createIndexerAwareClient(source: NodeProtocolInterface, indexerClient: HttpIndexerClient, indexers: IndexerDefinition[]): NodeProtocolInterface`
  - Wraps a source client
  - `read()` and `list()` check `inferViewFromUri()` and route to indexer when possible
  - `receive()` and `delete()` pass through to source
- [ ] Tests: version mismatch triggers rebuild, version match skips rebuild, inferViewFromUri matching, indexer-aware client routing

## Implementation Guide

1. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/evolution.ts`:
   - `initializeIndexer()`: read version from metadata store, compare with definition.version, orchestrate rebuild if needed
   - Use `backfillIndexer()` from G1-A6-05 for rebuild
2. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/routing.ts`:
   - `inferViewFromUri()`: iterate indexers, check `uriPatterns.some(p => uri.startsWith(p))`
   - `createIndexerAwareClient()`: use `FunctionalClient` pattern from `libs/b3nd-core/functional-client.ts`
3. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/evolution_test.ts`
4. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/evolution.ts` -- new: schema evolution logic
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/routing.ts` -- new: inferViewFromUri and indexer-aware client
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/evolution_test.ts` -- new: tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add exports

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/evolution_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-05 (backfill mechanism)
- **Blocks**: Nothing
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
